description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese katana, featuring a gently curved, single-edged blade housed within a black lacquered wooden sheath (saya). The sheath is adorned with subtle gold and red decorative markings, and a cord wrap is visible near the mouth of the sheath. The handle (tsuka) is wrapped in a dark material, likely ray skin and cord, and capped with a gold-colored pommel. A circular hand guard (tsuba) separates the handle from the blade, providing both protection and balance. The overall craftsmanship suggests a blend of functional design and aesthetic detail, typical of ceremonial or high-quality swords. The object is slender, elongated, and appears to be constructed primarily from metal and wood with decorative accents.
description: A traditional Japanese katana with a black lacquered sheath, gold and red markings, and a wrapped handle with a circular guard.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the katana in its sheath, highlighting the curved blade, decorative markings, and cord wrap.
description_view_1: End-on view showing the circular cross-section of the sheath and the gold pommel at the handle's end.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the curvature of the sheath and handle, with less visible ornamentation.
description_view_3: Reverse end-on view focusing on the sheath's tip and the alignment of the handle and guard.
attribute_handle_design: wrapped, straight handle with cord and gold pommel
attribute_ornamentation: gold and red markings on sheath
